title: Polynomiography
subtitle: Portraits from the Complex Plane
description: Every pixel is a window into the complex plane. Polynomial coefficients trace paths through space — circles, spirals, noise fields — while the Ehrlich–Aberth solver hunts for roots at every step. Color maps the invisible architecture of convergence: the boundaries where one root’s basin meets another, rendered as luminous landscapes of pure mathematics.

pages.title: Six Roots, Four Colors
pages.text: Degree 6. Seven coefficients wander the complex plane on figure-eight curves, a dithered circle, a disk spiral, and a twelve-spoke star with seventeen turns. The Ehrlich–Aberth solver finds all six roots at each of 99,900 sample points. Each pixel is colored by which root it converges to nearest, using a four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—modulated by relative proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Serpentine Turbulence
pages.text: Degree 18. Nineteen coefficients arrayed on the unit circle, most held stationary, with one tracing a hex grid under uniform dither, another scattered across a vast dithered circle, and one sweeping a two-spoke serpentine grid of 6,840 points. The Ehrlich–Aberth solver finds all eighteen roots, colored by derivative magnitude through the turbo palette—a continuous spectrum from deep blue through green and yellow to red. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Three-Spoke Derivative
pages.text: Degree 18. The same unit-circle arrangement of nineteen coefficients, but now one coefficient follows a three-spoke star across 9,850 points while another explores a hex grid with disk dither. Derivative magnitude maps to the turbo palette, revealing the rate of change of the polynomial across the complex plane. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Perlin Million
pages.text: Degree 18. One coefficient traverses a Perlin-noise-warped grid of 999,000 sample points while fifteen others orbit on tiny circles at staggered speeds. The four-color palette—red, near-black, deep blue, and emerald—assigns each pixel by which of the eighteen roots it converges to nearest, modulated by relative proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Dark Lattice
pages.text: Degree 18. A Perlin-warped grid path drives one coefficient through 99,900 sample points. Most of the remaining eighteen coefficients sit fixed on the unit circle; a few orbit on small circles with varying speeds and tilts. The four-color palette—red, near-black, deep blue, emerald—colors each pixel by nearest-root convergence. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Five Roots, Three Spokes
pages.text: Degree 5. Six coefficients: the leading one orbits on a tilted circle, two sit stationary, one traces a slow tilted orbit, one covers a Perlin-noise hex grid of 9,990 points with disk dither, and one sweeps a three-spoke star of 4,360 points. The four-color palette—red, near-black, deep blue, emerald—colors the five roots by proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Spokes and Hexagons
pages.text: Degree 5. The leading coefficient radiates along a three-spoke star of 4,360 points while another samples a Perlin-warped hex grid of 9,990 points with disk dither. Two more trace tilted circular orbits at different radii, and two sit stationary. The four-color palette—red, near-black, deep blue, emerald—colors the five roots by proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Elkal Rings
pages.text: Degree 18. The leading coefficient follows an elkal path—191 concentric rings spiraling inward with three-quarter-turn inner windings across 6,840 points—while a vast dithered circle scatters another coefficient and an annulus cloud distributes 9,990 points through a ring-shaped region. Relative proximity determines the four-color assignment—amber, green, blue, magenta—producing smooth gradients between root basins. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Double Elkal
pages.text: Degree 18. Two coefficients each trace elkal paths—250-ring spirals with 1.4-turn inner windings—while two others scatter across vast dithered circles and an annulus cloud casts 9,990 points through a ring. Relative proximity assigns four colors—amber, green, blue, magenta—across the eighteen root basins. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Hex Grid and Spiral
pages.text: Degree 6. Seven coefficients: three orbit on circles of varying radii, one samples a disk-dithered circle, one covers a hex grid of 20,000 points with disk dither, one spirals outward through a disk of 99,900 points over seventeen turns, and one sits stationary. The four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—colors the six roots by proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Spiral Unfurling
pages.text: Degree 6. The same seven-coefficient arrangement—circles, a dithered circle, a hex grid, and a seventeen-turn disk spiral of 99,900 points—captured at a different stage of the animation. Fewer spiral turns have accumulated, revealing the early structure of the root basins as they begin to interweave. The four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—colors by proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Twin Spirals
pages.text: Degree 8. Nine coefficients: two trace disk spirals—one tight with 30 turns across 9,990 points, one vast with the same winding but a far greater reach—while the leading coefficient orbits a small circle, one scatters a dithered circle, and four sit stationary. The four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—maps eight roots by proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Accelerated Spirals
pages.text: Degree 8. The same twin-spiral configuration but with faster coefficient speeds, producing denser sampling. Two disk spirals of 30 turns wind at accelerated pace while a dithered circle scatters broadly. The four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—maps eight roots by proximity. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Quintic Turbo
pages.text: Degree 5. Six coefficients: the leading one sits far from the origin, one traces a vast figure-eight curve of 200,000 points, one scatters across a dithered circle with normal dither, two orbit on tilted circles at vastly different scales, and one sits stationary. Derivative magnitude maps to the turbo palette with frequency modulation at 9.4 cycles, producing banded color rings that reveal the polynomial’s rate of change. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, JavaScript solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Golden Spiral
pages.text: Degree 5. Six coefficients on the unit circle: one traces a golden spiral of 999 points, one scatters across a dithered circle with normal dither, and four sit stationary. Derivative magnitude maps to the turbo palette with 9.4-cycle frequency modulation, revealing concentric bands of the polynomial’s rate of change radiating from the roots. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Golden Spiral, Relative Proximity
pages.text: Degree 5. The same golden-spiral configuration—one coefficient tracing 999 points outward, one scattering across a broadly dithered circle, four stationary—but now colored by relative proximity rather than derivative. The four-color palette—crimson, purple, blue, sage—assigns each pixel by which of the five roots it converges to nearest, with smooth gradients where basins of attraction meet. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Hex Grid Derivative
pages.text: Degree 7. Eight coefficients scattered across the complex plane, several far from the origin. One traverses a vast hex grid of 20,000 points, one scatters across a dithered circle with normal dither, and the rest sit stationary at varied positions. Derivative magnitude maps to the turbo palette with 5-cycle frequency modulation, producing concentric spectral bands around each of the seven roots. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Blue-White-Red Grid
pages.text: Degree 7. Eight coefficients scattered far across the complex plane. One traverses a vast hex grid of 20,000 points with three spokes, one scatters across a dithered circle with normal dither, and one spreads through a disk grid with heavy disk dither. Derivative magnitude maps to the blue–white–red palette with 5-cycle frequency modulation, producing cool-to-warm banded rings around each of the seven roots. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Dispersed Grid
pages.text: Degree 7. The same eight-coefficient arrangement as Blue–White–Red Grid but with vastly increased dither: the dithered circle expands to a radius of 500,000 with sigma 1,000, and the disk grid’s dither swells to sigma 47,400—scattering sample points across enormous regions of the complex plane. The hex grid still traverses 20,000 points with three spokes. Derivative magnitude maps to the blue–white–red palette with 5-cycle frequency modulation, now revealing finer structure as the wider sampling smooths the root basins. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Earth Palette
pages.text: Degree 5. Six coefficients on the unit circle: one samples a hex grid of 1,000 points with fine uniform dither, one orbits a tilted circle at moderate radius, one sweeps a wide tilted circle of radius 30 across 200 points, and three sit stationary. An eight-color earthy palette—sand, olive, sage, green, maroon, brown, purple, pink—assigns each pixel by which of the five roots it converges to nearest. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JavaScript solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Spiral Corners
pages.text: Degree 29. Thirty coefficients arranged in a six-by-five grid pattern. The four corner coefficients trace outward spirals with two turns each, while the remaining twenty-six sit stationary at their grid positions. Each of the twenty-nine roots receives a distinct rainbow hue, producing a vivid mosaic of interlocking basins of attraction. Trails enabled. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Sixty-Two Roots
pages.text: Degree 62. Sixty-three coefficients arrayed on a circle: the leading one orbits a tilted circle across 10,000 points, four scatter through disk clouds of 10,000 points each at varying radii and tilts, and the remaining fifty-eight sit stationary. Proximity to the nearest of sixty-two roots maps to the turbo palette with 5.8-cycle frequency modulation, producing intricate spectral rings around each basin of attraction. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JavaScript solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Rainbow Grid
pages.text: Degree 29. Thirty coefficients in a six-by-five grid, the same arrangement as Spiral Corners. The four corner coefficients trace outward spirals with two turns each while the remaining twenty-six hold their grid positions. Each of the twenty-nine roots receives a distinct rainbow hue. Trails enabled, capturing the history of root motion as the spirals unwind. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JavaScript solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Contrasting Spirals
pages.text: Degree 8. Nine coefficients on the unit circle: one traces a vast disk spiral of radius 999 with 30 turns across 9,990 points, another winds through a microscopic 30-turn spiral, one drifts on a tiny slow circle, one scatters a dithered circle with disk dither, and four sit stationary. The four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—assigns each pixel by which of the eight roots it converges to nearest, with 10-cycle frequency modulation producing fine concentric bands. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Contrasting Spirals II
pages.text: Degree 8. The same nine-coefficient arrangement as Contrasting Spirals—a vast 30-turn disk spiral, a microscopic 30-turn spiral, a slow drifting circle, a dithered circle, and four stationary—captured at a later stage of the animation. The large spiral has wound further outward, reshaping the basins of attraction. The four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—with 10-cycle frequency modulation. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Contrasting Spirals III
pages.text: Degree 8. A third frame from the Contrasting Spirals sequence. The vast disk spiral continues to expand while the microscopic spiral and dithered circle accumulate samples. The evolving root positions produce a different pattern of basin boundaries and color bands. The four-color palette—green, indigo, yellow, orange—with 10-cycle frequency modulation. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Warm Clusters
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients arranged in two clusters, every one tracing an outward ellipse of enormous radius with width 50 at the same speed. The two-cluster pattern places the coefficients along two arcs in the complex plane, all spiraling outward in lockstep. Derivative magnitude maps to the warm palette, rendering the polynomial’s rate of change in glowing reds, oranges, and yellows. Trails enabled. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Rainbow Twins
pages.text: Degree 19. Twenty coefficients in two symmetric clusters of ten, arranged along opposing arcs. One coefficient samples a hex grid of 9,990 points with fine disk dither, another orbits a circle of 2,000 points, and the remaining eighteen sit stationary. Each of the nineteen roots receives a distinct rainbow hue, producing a kaleidoscopic mosaic of interlocking basins mirrored across the two clusters. Black canvas, Hungarian root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Cloud Eleven
pages.text: Degree 11. Twelve coefficients scattered far across the complex plane: two drift through vast disk clouds of radius 272, one scatters a square cloud at radius 33, and the remaining nine sit stationary in two loose groups. Relative proximity to the nearest of eleven roots maps to the turbo palette with gentle 1.2-cycle frequency modulation, producing broad spectral gradients where basins of attraction overlap. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Four-Color Grid
pages.text: Degree 25. Twenty-six coefficients in a grid pattern: four trace hex grids of 999 points at staggered speeds, three orbit tilted dithered circles with disk dither, and the remaining nineteen sit stationary at their grid positions. Relative proximity assigns the four-color palette—amber, green, blue, magenta—with 8.2-cycle frequency modulation, producing fine concentric bands around each of the twenty-five root basins. Trails enabled. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Proximity Neon I
pages.text: Degree 25. Twenty-six coefficients in a grid pattern: four trace hex grids of 9990 points at radius 10, three orbit dithered circles at radius 100, two follow plain circles, and the remaining seventeen sit stationary. Proximity to the nearest root maps through a four-color neon palette—red, green-cyan, cyan, purple—with 10-cycle frequency modulation, producing tight luminous bands that ripple outward from each basin centre. Trails enabled. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Proximity Neon II
pages.text: Same configuration as Proximity Neon I captured ninety seconds later. The hex-grid and dithered-circle orbits have advanced, shifting the interference pattern and producing a different lattice of neon contours across the twenty-five root basins.

pages.title: Solar Disc
pages.text: Degree 25. Twenty-six coefficients in a grid pattern with tight orbits: four trace hex grids of roughly a thousand points at radius 1, one dithered circle sweeps a wide arc at radius 100, two plain circles orbit at radii 7 and 10, and the remaining nineteen sit stationary. The small hex-grid radius keeps all coefficient motion close to the grid, producing a compact glowing disc with lobed corona. Four-color proximity palette—red, green-cyan, cyan, purple—at 10-cycle frequency. Trails enabled. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Spectral Annulus
pages.text: A large dark ring floats on black, its outer and inner edges fringed with scalloped spectral bands—amber, green, cyan, blue—that repeat in ornate lobes around the circumference. A small red star glows faintly at the centre. Degree-25 polynomial root basins form the ring geometry, with trails accumulating the delicate colour gradients.

pages.title: Thermal Eye
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients on a circle pattern with wildly varied orbits: one annulus cloud sweeps radius 50 million, two spokes paths reach similar scales, a hex grid spans 5 million, a circle-dither at 500 thousand, a figure-eight at 5 thousand, a horizontal dither at 500, and the remaining twenty-four sit stationary. Derivative sensitivity maps through the turbo palette, producing concentric thermal rings—a red-orange outer glow fading to a dark gap, then a bright cyan ring encircling a warm inner disc. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JS solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Iris
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients on a circle pattern with extreme orbits: four hex grids and an annulus cloud at radii from 4 to 50 million, four spokes paths up to 50 million, two circle-dithers at 50 and 500 thousand, a horizontal dither at 500, and the remaining nineteen sit stationary. Derivative sensitivity maps through the blue-white-red palette with 7-cycle frequency modulation, producing a luminous pink disc with a dark pupil ringed by a violet-white corona of fine radial filaments. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Crowned Eclipse
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing enabled: twenty-nine sit stationary while two trace figure-eight-dither paths—one at radius 10 near the upper plane, another at radius 500 below centre. Derivative sensitivity maps through the turbo palette with 7-cycle frequency modulation, producing a vast dark disc with a luminous green-yellow core flanked by two dark pupils. An ornate scalloped corona of red, amber, green, and violet fractal lobes encircles the disc, thickening into a molten red bloom on one flank. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JS solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Violet Torus
pages.text: Degree 30. Sixty-two coefficients on a circle pattern: twenty-nine orbit on circles at staggered speeds, two trace figure-eight-dither paths, and thirty-one sit stationary—all at radius 9.99. Relative proximity maps through a custom eight-color neon palette—red, yellow, lime, cyan, aqua, blue, purple, magenta—producing a luminous cyan-violet torus threaded with moiré interference bands. A dark void opens at the centre, and a small orange ring with a dark pupil sits on the left rim where two basins of attraction meet. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JS solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Green Spore
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing enabled: twenty-nine trace outward-ellipse-dither paths at radius 0.5 with 2,000 points, disk dither, and width 50, at gradually increasing speeds, while one outlier orbits a circle of radius 5,000 far off-screen. Derivative sensitivity maps through the turbo palette with 6.1-cycle frequency modulation, producing a luminous lime-green spore with three dark lobes at its core. Radial filaments stream outward through an orange-red ring into a fiery corona of yellow, blue, and red striations, with a curling cyan tendril at one flank. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JS solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Neon Butterfly
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients in two clusters with morphing enabled. Sixteen coefficients trace dithered circles at radius 999 with 2,000 points and disk dither, speeds staggered from 0.001 to 0.299. Fifteen more spiral outward on two-turn dithered spirals of 5,664 points with normal dither, at similarly staggered speeds. The two sweeping red arcs form a butterfly figure, crossing at a luminous core where a bright cyan ring encircles a dark void. A corona of green scalloped lobes and magenta-purple filaments radiates between the arcs. The custom eight-color neon palette—red, orange, lime, green, cyan, blue, purple, magenta—assigns each pixel by proximity with gamma 0.5, producing vivid spectral gradients across the thirty root basins. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, JS solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Cardioid Bloom
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients in two clusters with morphing enabled. Sixteen coefficients sit at their home positions on dithered circles of radius 999 with 2,000 points and disk dither, speeds staggered from 0.001 to 0.299. Fifteen more rest at home on two-turn outward spirals of 5,664 points with normal dither at matching speeds. A broad red-orange cardioid arc sweeps from the upper left and curls inward, enclosing a luminous magenta-violet basin that fades to deep purple at the edges. At the centre, a small three-lobed red ring surrounds a dark pupil fringed with green and cyan scallops. A rainbow gradient—green, yellow, orange, red—bleeds from the left edge where multiple root basins converge. The custom eight-color neon palette—red, orange, lime, green, cyan, blue, purple, magenta—assigns each pixel by proximity with gamma 0.5. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Concentric Chains
pages.text: Degree 30. Thirty-one coefficients in two clusters with morphing enabled. Sixteen coefficients sit at their home positions on dithered circles of radius 999 with 2,000 points and disk dither, speeds staggered from 0.001 to 0.299. Fifteen more rest at home on two-turn outward spirals of 5,664 points with normal dither at matching speeds. A luminous red-orange ring floats at the centre, surrounded by a pale blue halo and a dark void. Concentric chains of tiny multicoloured loops—cyan, green, yellow, red—radiate outward in delicate lacework, each chain tracing a different orbit of the thirty root basins. The custom eight-color neon palette with proximity frequency modulation at 8.4 cycles produces the fine banding within each chain. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Dithered Nebula
pages.text: Degree 5 expanded by seventeen pinned roots. Six coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing enabled along a figure-eight C–D path. Two coefficients orbit on tilted circles—one at radius 1 with speed 0.5 and tilt 0.33, the other at radius 0.3 with speed 0.85 and tilt 0.85—while four sit stationary. The D-node for the fifth coefficient traverses a hex grid of 999,000 points at radius 33,500, far off in the complex plane, while two other D-nodes rest at distant positions. Strong Gaussian morph dither at 0.8 sigma mid-path scatters the interpolated coefficients along the figure-eight, producing a turbulent organic structure. Seventeen pinned roots trace a curved arc, expanding the quintic to an effective degree of 22. A deep blue-violet core, threaded with fine concentric moiré patterns, fades outward through cyan and green into a bright yellow-green aureole. Red-orange flame-like tendrils with jagged serrated edges extend to the left and right, curling back at the tips. The custom eight-color neon palette—red, amber, lime, green, cyan, blue, purple, magenta—assigns each pixel by proximity. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Four-Lobed Bloom
pages.text: Degree 5 expanded by seventeen pinned roots. Six coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing along a figure-eight C–D path and scale-center jiggle applied to all six. Two C-nodes orbit on tilted circles while four sit stationary. Three D-nodes now carry their own paths: one traces a 57-ring elkal spiral of 9,990 points at radius 500, another covers a hex grid of 99,900 points at radius 3,350, and the third radiates along three spokes of 2,000 points at radius 500. Gaussian morph dither at 2.0 sigma mid-path—more than double the previous frame—scatters the interpolated coefficients heavily along the figure-eight, while the scale-center jiggle slowly pushes all six coefficients outward from the origin. The result is a compact four-lobed flower: a luminous multicoloured core of red, cyan, green, and blue gives way to four dark petals, each outlined by sweeping red-orange arcs that overlap and interweave like magnetic field lines. The dark voids between the lobes deepen toward the edges. The custom eight-color neon palette—red, amber, lime, green, cyan, blue, purple, magenta—assigns each pixel by proximity. No trails. Black canvas, assign-four root matching, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Peacock Chrysanthemum
pages.text: Degree 18. Nineteen coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing along a C+D path and scale-center jiggle. Two C-nodes carry their own paths—one on a hex grid of 2,000 points at radius 10, another on a dithered grid-serpentine of 9,990 points at radius 50 with disk dither—while seventeen sit stationary. Six D-nodes have paths: D₀ orbits a circle of enormous radius 50,000 with 200 points, D₄ and D₁₀ each orbit circles of radius 9,990, D₇ radiates along seven spokes of 999 points at radius 10 with speed 0.336, D₁₄ traverses a grid-serpentine of 999 points at radius 1, and D₁₇ orbits a circle of radius 999 at speed 0.285. No morph dither. Morph rate 0.52, mu near 0.93. Roughly eighteen crimson petals radiate outward from the centre, each textured with fine filamentary striations that dissolve into pale hairlike strands at the tips. A mid-ring of circular “eye” motifs in sky blue and cyan, each with a red centre ring and golden-yellow surround, encircles a darker turbulent core threaded with olive and yellow. Proximity frequency modulation at 6.1 cycles and relative-proximity frequency at 8.1 cycles produce the fine concentric banding within each petal and eye. The custom four-colour palette—crimson, sage green, golden yellow, sky blue—assigns each pixel by proximity with Hungarian matching. No trails. Black canvas, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers, target time 40.5 seconds.

pages.title: Ascending Flare
pages.text: Degree 18. Nineteen coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing along a C+D path and scale-center jiggle—same base configuration as the previous frame but with four additional D-node paths pushed to extreme radii. D₁ now covers a disk grid of 9,990 points at radius 5,000; D₁₅ sweeps a vertical line of 999 points at radius 999,000,000; D₁₆ sweeps a horizontal line of 999 points at radius 50,000,000; D₁₈ radiates along eight spokes of 9,990 points at radius 500. The enormous vertical and horizontal D-node radii pull the C+D morph interpolation to astronomical distances, collapsing most of the eighteen root basins into a single luminous structure in the upper half of the frame. A crimson starburst with roughly eighteen pointed rays surrounds a bright golden-yellow core, wrapped in a sage-green halo that fades to dark olive. Fine filamentary strands spray outward like a dandelion head. From the flame’s apex a narrow cyan-blue and crimson plume jets upward, curving slightly at the tip where the extreme vertical D-node distorts the basin boundary. The lower half of the canvas is nearly black. The custom four-colour palette—crimson, sage green, golden yellow, sky blue—assigns each pixel by proximity with Hungarian matching. Proximity frequency 6.1, relative-proximity frequency 8.1. No trails. Black canvas, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers, target time 40.5 seconds.

pages.title: Aurora Canopy
pages.text: Degree 18 with trails enabled. Nineteen coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing along a line C–D path and scale-center jiggle. Several paths upgraded to elkal spirals (8 rings, 0.5 inner turns, 0.5 inner ratio): C₁ traces an elkal of 2,000 points at radius 10, C₁₇ an elkal of 9,990 points at radius 50. On the D side, D₀ spirals an elkal of 200 points at radius 50,000, D₄ and D₁₀ each trace elkals of 200 points at radius 9,990, D₁₄ an elkal of 999 points at radius 1 at speed 0.408, and D₁₇ an elkal of 999 points at radius 999 at speed 0.285. D₁ radiates five spokes of 9,990 points at radius 9,990. D₁₅ sweeps a vertical line at radius 999,000,000 and D₁₆ a horizontal line at radius 50,000,000—pushing the line-path morph to extreme distances. D₁₈ radiates eight spokes of 9,990 points at radius 500. Root trails record the motion of all eighteen roots, painting dense flowing curves across the frame. The derivative colour mode with the turbo palette maps |P′(z)| magnitude to a rainbow spectrum. The result resembles a luminous canopy: horizontal bands of green, yellow, and orange stream across the upper half in layered waves, while below, tangled spiralling tendrils in cyan, blue, and violet curl and twist like roots of a great tree. Thin red-orange crests along the top edge recall an aurora. The lower third fades to black. Hungarian matching, no morph dither. Black canvas, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers, target time 40.5 seconds.

pages.title: Molten Torus
pages.text: Degree 18. Nineteen coefficients on a circle pattern with morphing along a C+D path at rate 1 and scale-center jiggle (sigma 0.11, scaleStep 1). Prolonged jiggle has pushed all coefficients to positions in the millions. C₁ traces a 250-ring elkal spiral of 2,000 points at the enormous radius of 999,000,000 at speed 0.251. C₁₇ traces a dithered 250-ring elkal of 9,990 points at radius 50,000,000 with disk dither at sigma 100 and speed 0.556. On the D side, most nodes carry elkal spirals at various radii: D₀ at radius 50,000 with 9,990 points, D₁ at radius 5,000,000, D₂ at 50,000, D₃ at 500,000, D₄ at 999,000 at speed 0.22, and D₇ at 99,900 at speed 0.278—all with 8 rings. Light morph dither at 0.02 sigma mid-path. The derivative colour mode with the turbo palette and frequency modulation at 5.7 cycles maps |P′(z)| to smooth rainbow gradients. A luminous torus floats against black: a broad vermilion-orange inner band encircles a dark void with a fine sawtooth inner edge, while the outer rim is textured with turbulent swirls of yellow, olive, and lavender. A bright chartreuse glow bleeds from the right where a root basin boundary pinches inward. Hungarian matching. No trails. Black canvas, JS solver on sixteen parallel workers, target time 40.5 seconds.

pages.title: Filament Ring
pages.text: Degree 18. Same extreme-scale coefficient configuration as the previous frame but with D-node paths switched from elkal spirals to twelve-spoke radiations: D₀ through D₄ and D₇ all radiate twelve spokes of 9,990 points at radius 50,000 at speed 0.163. C₁₇ also switches from dithered elkal to twelve spokes at radius 50,000,000. Morph dither increases sharply to 0.45 sigma mid-path (from 0.02), scattering the C+D interpolated coefficients across the disk distribution. The derivative colour mode with the turbo palette and 5.7-cycle frequency modulation colours each pixel by |P′(z)|. The result is a more delicate, threadlike ring: fine filamentary strands in orange, yellow, green, and blue weave around a dark central void with faint radial spoke structures visible inside. A red tendril curls away from the right side of the ring. Scattered chartreuse dots trace curved arcs across the black field—stray basin boundaries from the astronomical coefficient positions. The higher dither dissolves the smooth torus of the previous frame into a luminous, fibrous halo. Hungarian matching. No trails. Black canvas, JS solver on sixteen parallel workers, target time 40.5 seconds.

pages.title: Luna Moth
pages.text: Degree 5 with all six coefficients on spokes paths (rAbs ≈ 100, C₁ at 999). D-nodes at unit-hexagon vertices with line morph path and disk dither (σ = 2.7). Custom 4-colour relative-proximity colouring with trails. The dithered line morph sweeps coefficients between C and D positions, and the spokes animation fans them outward, producing a paired-wing silhouette of luminous green filaments against black.

pages.title: Jewelled Cardioid
pages.text: Degree 30 with 31 static C-nodes equally spaced on the unit circle (rAbs = 0.5). All D-nodes on hex-grid paths (rAbs = 5) with line morph and heavy square dither (σ = 10). Custom 4-colour relative-proximity colouring with trails. The grid-walking D-nodes scatter the morph targets across a lattice, producing a swirl of bright multicoloured beads that trace a cardioid-like void ringed by prismatic spray.

pages.title: Open Book
pages.text: Degree 6 with mixed C-node paths: two circles (C₀ and C₃), one hex-grid-dither (C₂, rAbs ≈ 8.5), and three static nodes. D-nodes at C-node (morph rate 0.01) with Hungarian matching and custom 4-colour proximity colouring. Trails accumulate the stippled output of the hex-grid dither into ruled arcs, while the orbiting circles sweep feathered wings above—evoking two doves rising from the pages of an illuminated manuscript.

pages.title: Celestial Eye
pages.text: Degree 6, WASM solver. Three circles (C₀, C₃, C₆) orbit while two hex-grid-dither nodes (C₁ at rAbs ≈ 854, C₂ at rAbs ≈ 8.5) scatter stippled points across wide lattices. C-node morph path with Hungarian matching and custom 4-colour proximity colouring. Trails weave the orbital sweeps and grid scatter into a luminous iris—a glowing blue-red annulus ringed by tangled filament streamers, with a dark pupil at its centre.

pages.title: Copper Windmill
pages.text: Degree 5 with D×C morph path and scale-center jiggle. Six C-nodes on circle paths; all six D-nodes placed at unit-hexagon vertices on three-spoke paths (rAbs 999, 9,990 points each). The D×C complex product multiplies each coefficient's position by its morph target, folding the spokes radiation into a rotationally distorted field. Derivative magnitude maps through the turbo palette with 9.5-cycle frequency modulation. Four broad copper-brown blades sweep outward from the centre in a slow pinwheel, separated by luminous amber-orange channels textured with fine directional brushstrokes. At the hub, a bright cyan-green rosette with four lobes surrounds a tiny dark pupil—the only cool tones in the frame. Hungarian matching, no trails. Black canvas, WASM solver on sixteen parallel workers.

pages.title: Spectral Nebula
pages.text: Degree 5 with C/D morph path—complex division of each C-node position by its D-node target. The division's singularity near D≈0 hurls root basins outward in violent asymmetric plumes while the quotient's phase twists them into concentric ripple bands. A luminous core of pale lavender and silver resolves into fine moiré interference fringes at upper left; the lower hemisphere shatters into a crystalline debris field of sharp-edged facets and dark voids. Faint orbital arcs—ghostly ellipses from the C-node circle paths—lace the outer darkness. Proximity colouring through a muted grey-violet-gold palette, WASM solver on sixteen workers.

pages.title: Scattered Jewels
pages.text: Degree 5 with Möbius morph path (aC+D)/(C+aD). Each root basin fragments into dozens of tiny luminous squares—emerald, ruby, sapphire, amber—scattered across the left half of the frame like gemstones spilled on black velvet. A vertical curtain of soft teal-green glow anchors the left edge while a dense cluster of multicoloured pixel confetti drifts rightward into a pale silvery wash, dissolving into darkness at the margins. The Möbius transform's conformal warping compresses some basins into tight jewel-like facets and stretches others into diffuse hazes, creating an asymmetric constellation that reads simultaneously as particle field and dim galaxy. WASM solver, sixteen workers.

pages.title: Cosmic Egg
pages.text: Degree 5 with geometric interpolation C^a·D^(1−a). The log-polar blending spirals root basins into a massive dark void at centre, ringed by a molten orange-brown corona. Saturated colour fields—amber, vermillion, cobalt, lime green—radiate outward in broad brushed sectors, their boundaries scored by fine directional hatching from the solver's convergence gradients. Inside the dark bowl, a delicate olive-green filigree of spiral arms and stippled lace curls inward, dotted with a few bright chartreuse lobes where basin boundaries pile up. The geometric mean's tendency to magnify phase differences at small magnitudes carves the interior into intricate lacework while the exterior stays bold and painterly. Custom multi-colour proximity palette, WASM solver on sixteen workers.

pages.title: Chromatic Eclipse
pages.text: Degree 11 with trails. Twelve coefficients all on disk-spiral paths in two tiers: C₀–C₅ at rAbs 50,000 with graduated speeds (0.09–0.25), C₆–C₁₁ at rAbs 9,990,000 with matched speed 0.443. The enormous radius gap—two hundred to one—flings the higher-order terms through vast arcs while the lower terms drift gently, splitting the derivative field into two complementary hemispheres: acid chartreuse at lower left, saturated violet-blue at upper right. A massive black void occupies the centre, its rim traced by a fine stippled halo of convergence boundary dust. Across the void's waist, a bridge of copper-orange filaments fans from a luminous pinch point—the solver's convergence gradients compressed where the two colour fields collide. Faint red thread-like traces wander the chartreuse field, ghosts of earlier spiral passes. Derivative magnitude through the turbo palette, custom 4-colour RP4 (amber, green, red, magenta), assign4 matching, black canvas.

pages.title: Woven Dusk
pages.text: Degree 20 with 21 coefficients split between vertical and horizontal paths. Eleven vertical movers and ten horizontal movers at rAbs 9.99 with gentle, graduated speeds weave the twenty root basins into a fibrous lattice—lavender-purple warp threads crossing sage-green weft. Where the two families intersect the proximity field compresses into a taut grid of dark voids framed by luminous filament crossings; at the edges the strands splay outward into feathery sprays, each individual root trajectory resolving as a fine hair-like trace. The orthogonal path geometry imposes a textile structure on the complex plane: a shimmering swatch of algorithmic fabric suspended in black space. Custom 8-colour proximity palette (inferno base with browns, greens, cyans, blues, purples), assign4 matching, black canvas, WASM solver on sixteen workers.

pages.title: Spiral Rosary
pages.text: Degree 30 with 31 coefficients uniformly spaced on the unit circle, every one following a disk-spiral-dither path at rAbs 5, speed 0.227, with Gaussian dither (sigma 0.0248). Morph enabled at rate 0.01 with c-node CD paths—the morph targets sit stationary at each coefficient's home position, so the solver field breathes gently around the dithered spirals. The result is a luminous necklace of concentric bullseye beads—teal-cyan halos around terracotta-orange cores—strung along a sweeping open arc against pure black. At the top of the arc the beads shrink and compress into a smooth gradient curl of green and coral that tightens into a smaller teardrop hook, a faint red tendril trailing from the inner whorl. The thirty evenly-spaced coefficients impose the bead periodicity; the dither clouds each bead's basin boundary into soft concentric rings rather than hard edges. Custom 4-colour proximity palette (dusty rose, sage green, dark teal, dark purple), assign4 matching, gamma 0.5, black canvas, WASM solver on sixteen workers.
